mission.title: Install and Configure Kube-OVN
mission.description: Kube-OVN is an advanced Kubernetes network fabric that bridges SDN and Cloud Native, providing enterprise-level networking features including subnet management, static IPs, ACLs, QoS, and multi-cluster connectivity built on OVN/OVS. It is a CNCF Sandbox project with 2,300+ stars.

steps.title: Label control-plane nodes for OVN DB placement
steps.description:
Kube-OVN requires at least one node labeled for OVN database placement. Label your control-plane node(s).

```bash
kubectl label node -lnode-role.kubernetes.io/control-plane kube-ovn/role=master --overwrite
```

> This tells Kube-OVN where to schedule the OVN central components.

steps.title: Add the Kube-OVN Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Kube-OVN Helm chart repository.

```bash
helm repo add kubeovn https://kubeovn.github.io/kube-ovn/
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Kube-OVN using Helm
steps.description:
Install Kube-OVN into kube-system namespace. The default configuration uses geneve tunnels with 10.16.0.0/16 pod CIDR.

```bash
helm install kube-ovn kubeovn/kube-ovn --wait -n kube-system --version v1.16.0
```

> Adjust --version to match your desired release. See https://github.com/kubeovn/kube-ovn/releases for available versions.
commands: helm install kube-ovn kubeovn/kube-ovn --wait -n kube-system --version v1.16.0

steps.title: Verify Kube-OVN pods are running
steps.description:
Check that all Kube-OVN components are running in kube-system.

```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app=kube-ovn
```

> You should see kube-ovn-controller, kube-ovn-cni, kube-ovn-pinger, ovn-central, and ovs-ovn pods in Running state.

steps.title: Verify default subnet
steps.description:
Confirm the default subnet was created and is available.

```bash
kubectl get subnets
```

> You should see an 'ovn-default' subnet with CIDR 10.16.0.0/16 in the Available state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kube-OVN
uninstall.description:
Remove Kube-OVN from your cluster.

```bash
helm uninstall kube-ovn -n kube-system
```
